commit.message:
Fit all windows within 600 px height for small Haiku screens

- Reduced ExtractionWindow tips scroll area: 130→70 min, 180→90 max
- Reduced RoastColorWindow thumbnail: 320×240 → 320×180
- Reduced RoastColorWindow tips scroll: 130→70 min, 200→100 max
- Removed unnecessary 40px height buffer from all three windows' ResizeTo() calls
- Updated BRIEF.md to reflect thumbnail size changes and window optimization

All tool windows now fit within 600px total height (content + title bar + borders)
to accommodate smaller Haiku displays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

  with global-error.tsx (both previously exported GlobalError)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add --no-install-recommends to apt-get install (docker:S6500)

Reduces image attack surface by not installing unnecessary recommended
packages alongside gcc and libpq-dev in the backend Docker image.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

merge: integrate main (CAE sub-tabs + Railway overhaul) into sanarQubIssuesFIxes

Conflicts resolved:
- backend/Dockerfile: combined non-root user security fix with start.sh entrypoint,
  merged consecutive RUN instructions into one layer
- frontend/components/modules/cae-tab.tsx: took main's rewrite (sub-tabs + useToast),
  our alert() fix was already absorbed
- PROGRESS.md: manual merge, appended Etape 14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add type-check script and suppress S1128 for app pages

- Add `type-check` npm script (tsc --noEmit) to frontend/package.json
  to catch type-import regressions like the Phase 8 RequiredFieldsForm/CalendarView incident
- Suppress typescript:S1128 (unused imports) on frontend/app/** in sonar-project.properties
  to avoid false positives on JSX component imports that SonarCloud misidentifies as type-only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): rebuild HEMesh after push-pull so face splitting works

After push-pull creates/modifies geometry via Manifold, the HEMesh
becomes stale. Now we rebuild it from the new BufferGeometry so that
line-tool face splitting works on push-pull-created boxes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): build HEMesh directly at box creation, avoid lossy conversion

The round-trip BoxGeometry → HEMesh → BufferGeometry was corrupting
geometry. Now boxes are created with HEMesh as the source of truth,
and LineTool only attempts face splitting on objects that already
have an HEMesh (no more ensureHEMesh conversion).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model-3d): use point-in-face lookup instead of stale triangle index

ensureHEMesh() rebuilds the geometry from the HEMesh, which changes all
triangle indices. The targetFaceIndex captured during pickDrawingPlane
was stale by the time finalizeLine used it.

Fix: add findFaceAtPoint() which does a 3D point-on-plane check + 2D
point-in-polygon test to find the correct HE face from the line's start
point, independent of triangle indices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add comprehensive seed data demos with full pipeline runner

6 demo projects (45 input files + 43 expected files) covering all
supported languages and both cloud providers:
- aws_python_microservice: 9 files (S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, Secrets Manager, Lambda, IAM)
- aws_typescript_api: 6 files (@aws-sdk S3, DynamoDB, SQS)
- aws_terraform_infra: 11 files (Lambda, S3, DynamoDB, RDS, ElastiCache, SQS, SNS, IAM, KMS, CloudWatch, API Gateway, CloudFront, Route53)
- aws_cloudformation_stack: 3 files (VPC, S3/KMS, Lambda/DynamoDB/SQS/SNS/IAM)
- azure_python_webapp: 7 files (Blob Storage, Cosmos DB, Service Bus, Key Vault, Identity)
- azure_terraform_infra: 9 files (Functions, AKS, VMs, Storage, Cosmos DB, SQL, Service Bus, Event Hub)

Pipeline runner (demos/run_demos.py) exercises all 5 stages:
Parse → Detect → Match+Transform → Diff → Validate

Results: 202 detections, 109 transforms, 45/45 validated clean, 0 errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add context gathering step to all self-review prompts

Update prompts to require Agent to gather context before making decisions:

1. _build_self_review_prompt:

   - Step 1: Gather context (read feature_list.json, CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, git diff)

2. _build_memory_cleanup_prompt:
   - Added git context (branch, recent commits)
   - Step 1: Gather context (read MEMORY.md, feature_list.json, recent changes)
   - Step 2: Analyze (DELETE, UPDATE, ADD)
   - Step 3: Execute changes

3. _build_claude_md_cleanup_prompt:
   - Added git context (branch, recent commits)
   - Step 1: Gather context (read CLAUDE.md, feature_list.json, recent changes, MEMORY.md)
   - Step 2: Analyze (DELETE, UPDATE, ADD)

This ensures Agent makes informed decisions based on sufficient context

commit.message:
feat: MLB Sprint 2+3 — CatBoost V1 model, signal system, full pipeline

MLB Pitcher Strikeouts prediction system ready for 2026 season (Mar 27):

Sprint 2 (Signal & Best Bets):
- 8 active signals + 6 shadow + 4 negative filters
- Best bets exporter with UNDER signal-quality ranking
- Grading processor with MLB void logic (rain-shortened, IP conversion)
- Multi-system prediction support

Sprint 3 (Walk-Forward + Model Training):
- Walk-forward simulation: CatBoost 120d wins (54.2% HR at edge 1.0+)
- CatBoost V1 trained, all 5 governance gates passed (62.2% HR)
- XGBoost V1 shadow model registered
- Quick retrain script with governance gates

Session 429 fixes:
- Feature contract fix: pitcher_loader provides all 31 CatBoost features
- Dockerfile: added libgomp1 for CatBoost runtime
- Main scraper registry synced with 3 new MLB scrapers
- velocity_change formula corrected to match training

New scrapers: Statcast daily, MLB API box scores, Reddit discussion
New processors: statcast_daily, mlbapi_pitcher_stats, mlbapi_batter_stats
New schemas: 8 MLB prediction + raw table definitions

NBA: mean_reversion_under weight removed (cross-season decay to 53.0%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: GALA Playground

Web-based playground for the GALA programming language, similar to
Go Playground (go.dev/play). Users can write GALA code in a browser
editor and execute it.

Features:
- Dark-themed IDE-like UI with code editor and output panel
- 9 pre-loaded examples (sealed types, collections, pattern matching, etc.)
- Ctrl+Enter to run, resizable split panels, line numbers
- 30s execution timeout, code size limit
- Multi-stage Dockerfile with pre-warmed GALA stdlib
- Downloads GALA binary from GitHub releases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(cheats): add libretroCheatSystemName to resolve system name mismatches (#2642)

The libretro cheat database uses different directory names than the
thumbnail database for some systems. DOOM cheats live under "PrBoom"
(not "DOOM"), and Atari Jaguar CD cheats are under "Atari - Jaguar CD"
(not "Atari - Atari Jaguar" which does not exist).

Add libretroCheatSystemName computed property to SystemIdentifier that
returns the correct cht/ directory name for cheat lookups. Most systems
are unchanged (it defaults to libretroDatabaseName). Only DOOM and
AtariJaguarCD are overridden.

Update PVEmulatorViewController+Cheats to use the new property so
cheat searches correctly filter by system.

Part of #2617

Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <41898282+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Map standard LLM model names to realtime equivalents on backend

The OpenAI Realtime WebSocket only accepts realtime models. Added
LLM_TO_REALTIME_MAP so agent models like gpt-4o, gpt-4-turbo,
gpt-4o-mini, gpt-3.5-turbo are mapped to their best realtime
equivalent instead of falling back to the default.

gpt-4o / gpt-4-turbo -> gpt-4o-realtime-preview-2025-06-03
gpt-4o-mini / gpt-3.5-turbo -> gpt-4o-mini-realtime-preview-2024-12-17

commit.message:
Fix emoji favicons across all pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use accessToken for server-side Supabase client to fix RLS failures

The global.headers.Authorization approach was losing the JWT in the Edge
Runtime, causing all DB requests to run as the anon role. Switched to the
accessToken option and added tool capabilities to the system prompt so the
agent knows it can create groups.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix emoji favicons across all pages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: remove top-level vite import to fix Vercel 500 errors

Moved vite import to a dynamic import inside the dev-only block so it
is never loaded in the Vercel Lambda environment. Also moved route
registration out of the async startServer() to ensure routes are
available immediately when the module is imported by api/index.ts.

commit.message:
Fix flaky mixed read/write concurrency test

The assertion expected only size 0 or file_size, but write_bytes()
truncates then writes so concurrent readers can legitimately see any
intermediate size. Changed to 0 <= size <= file_size.

fix: 숏츠팩토리 영상 — CSP media-src 추가 + Supabase 영구 업로드

- next.config.mjs: media-src 지시어 추가 (vidgen.x.ai, *.supabase.co, blob:)
  → <video> 태그가 xAI 영상 URL을 재생할 수 있도록 CSP 허용
- shorts_factory.py: _upload_video_to_storage() 함수 추가
  → 이미지처럼 xAI 임시 URL을 Supabase Storage에 영구 저장
- _run_grok_video_step: 영상 생성 후 Supabase 업로드
- regenerate_video: 재생성 후 Supabase 업로드

repository.name: cubesystem
repository.description: company

commit.message:
Add chapter overview, character/place notes, and manuscript statistics

Feature 5 — Chapter Overview Panel (Ctrl+Shift+O): sidebar listing all
chapters per novel project with per-chapter word counts, total word
count, progress bar toward a configurable target (default 80k), and
estimated reading time. Click any chapter to open it.

Feature 6 — Character & Place Notes (Ctrl+Shift+N): sidebar panel with
Characters and Places tabs, add/edit/delete entries (name, one-line
description, expandable notes), quick search/filter, stored in Dexie
notes table per project.

Feature 7 — Manuscript Statistics (Ctrl+Shift+M or STATS in status
bar): modal showing total words, avg per chapter, reading time, words
today/week/month, daily average, projected finish date, personal best
day and week. Daily writing tracked every 30s in Dexie writingStats
table (incremental delta, words-only-go-up approach).

Also bumps Dexie DB to version 2 with notes and writingStats tables.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: send null password to leads RPC (matches get_analytics_v2 behavior)

app.analytics_password setting is null in Supabase, so sending an actual
password string causes IS DISTINCT FROM null = true = Unauthorized.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix custom.css 404 by using Hugo Pipes

relURL does not copy assets/ files to public. Use resources.Get + minify
to process through Hugo Pipes so the file is actually emitted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(vision): optimize denoising README and fix annotation imports

- Consolidate quick-reference commands with $RUN shell variable
- Fix model count 61→64 after deraining models were added
- Add rain/deraining branch to decision tree
- Separate rain examples in quick-reference with own comment block
- Document metrics.json vs metrics.jsonl for traditional baselines
- Add arch alias hint (unet++, nlm, tv, ddpm, dead_hot, etc.)
- Add from __future__ import annotations to fix TYPE_CHECKING runtime errors
